rationale.R1.1: The artifact carries a prominent conceptual tension: the paper is titled and framed around DialogueDev (a multi-agent protocol) yet mid-section declares RSP (a single-agent prompting strategy) as the 'central contribution.' Reconstructing which claim is actually load-bearing and whether the thesis is coherently stated is an expected reviewer task.
rationale.R1.2: The argument that prompting strategy dominates communication topology as a source of gains is a strong causal claim. Reviewing requires checking whether the intermediate steps — stripped-down single-agent + RSP recovering 'most' multi-agent performance — logically support the conclusion that agent decomposition is not the primary driver.
rationale.R1.3: Specific quantitative claims are asserted (RSP pass@1 78.4% vs 71.2% baseline; 34/80 vs 21/80 GitHub issues). Any review of this section should verify that the numbers cited are internally consistent and that the characterization of gains as 'largely orthogonal' and 'dominant variance' is grounded in the reported figures rather than overstated.

parsed.clarification_question: Should this review focus primarily on the scientific substance (claim validity, internal logical coherence, evidence alignment), or on writing quality (prose clarity, sentence-level polish, narrative flow)?
